content.text:
[
  {
    "idea_id": "sector-rotation-model",
    "title": "Sector Rotation Model",
    "thesis": "The sector rotation model aims to generate excess returns by rotating investments across different economic sectors based on their relative strength. This model seeks to capture sector-level trends and rotates investments to the strongest sectors.",
    "data_sources": [
      "yfinance",
      "FRED"
    ],
    "signal_logic": {
      "calculate_sector_momentum": "Calculate the 12-month price momentum for each sector ETF",
      "rank_sectors": "Rank the sectors from highest to lowest momentum",
      "select_top_sectors": "Select the top 3-5 sectors with the highest momentum"
    },
    "entry_exit": {
      "entry": "Invest equally in the top sectors identified",
      "exit": "Rebalance the portfolio monthly, rotating out of the weakest sectors and into the new top sectors"
    },
    "position_sizing": "Equal-weight the top sectors identified",
    "universe": [
      "XLY",
      "XLP",
      "XLE",
      "XLF",
      "XLV",
      "XLI",
      "XLB",
      "XLK",
      "XLU"
    ],
    "frequency": "monthly",
    "risks": {
      "sector_concentration_risk": "Exposure to a few sectors may lead to higher volatility",
      "momentum_reversal_risk": "Sectors with high momentum may experience a reversal, leading to losses"
    },
    "implementation_notes": {
      "1. Fetch sector ETF prices using yfinance",
      "2. Calculate 12-month momentum for each sector",
      "3. Rank sectors by momentum and select top 3-5",
      "4. Rebalance portfolio monthly to maintain equal weight in top sectors"
    },
    "required_keys": []
  }
]
